<!-- Generated by `go run ./cmd/neo-docs`. Do not edit by hand. -->

# Configuration

Neo loads the first available config:

1. `./neo.yaml`
2. `~/.neo/config.yaml`
3. Embedded defaults from `internal/config/defaults/neo.yaml`

First hit wins. Config files are not merged.

## Default Config

```yaml
# neo default configuration.
# Override by creating ./neo.yaml in a project, or ~/.neo/config.yaml globally.

model: claude-opus-4-8

# Optional, layered capabilities. Each defaults to on when omitted; set a flag
# to false to disable it. The core agent loop is never affected by these.
features:
  agents_file: true # load AGENTS.md (project tree + ~/.neo) into the system prompt
  skills: true      # discover .neo/skills, advertise them, expand $name references
  prompt_caching: true # cache_control the static system prompt prefix to cut input cost
```

## Feature Flags

Each feature flag is tri-state in Go: absent means use the built-in default, while explicit `false` disables that capability.

| Flag | Default | Effect |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `agents_file` | `true` | Load AGENTS.md into the chat system prompt. |
| `skills` | `true` | Discover skills and expand $name references. |
| `prompt_caching` | `true` | Mark the stable system prompt prefix as cacheable when the provider supports it. |
